req.title: Bulk data export (negative)
req.statement: A provisioned client shall request a FHIR bulk export; large datasets shall be paginated with NDJSON output. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-066 mandates FHIR bulk export via /$export with NDJSON pagination for authorised clients and a 403 for unauthorised ones. Five test cases are designed: (1) EP-positive: authorised client triggers export and receives 202 with Content-Location; (2) NEG: unauthorised client receives 403; (3) BVA: export with exactly 1 record produces a single-line NDJSON file (lower boundary of pagination); (4) STC: a second kick-off while a job is already in-flight must be rejected (conflicting state transition); (5) DT: request with a missing mandatory _outputFormat element triggers a 400. Each test is self-contained via fixtures and cleanup hooks.

tests.name: test_bulk_export_authorised_client_returns_202
preconditions: Client is provisioned with bulk-export scope
actions: POST /$export with Accept: application/fhir+json and Prefer: respond-async
Capture HTTP status code and Content-Location header
expected: Content-Location header is present and points to a polling URL
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_bulk_export_authorised_client_returns_202(fhir_base_url, authorised_headers):
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/$export",
        headers={
            **authorised_headers,
            "Accept": "application/fhir+json",
            "Prefer": "respond-async",
        },
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    assert "Content-Location" in resp.headers, "Content-Location header missing"
    polling_url = resp.headers["Content-Location"]
    assert polling_url.startswith("http"), f"Unexpected polling URL: {polling_url}"

tests.name: test_bulk_export_unauthorised_client_returns_403
preconditions: Client token lacks bulk-export scope or is entirely absent
actions: GET /$export with Accept: application/fhir+json, Prefer: respond-async and no/invalid Bearer token
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome resource

tests.name: test_bulk_export_single_record_produces_one_line_ndjson
preconditions: Exactly 1 Patient resource exists in the test tenant
Client is provisioned with bulk-export scope
actions: GET /$export?_type=Patient with authorised headers and Prefer: respond-async
Download the first NDJSON output URL from the manifest
Count newline-delimited lines in the NDJSON body
expected: Export manifest lists exactly 1 output file for Patient
NDJSON file contains exactly 1 non-empty line
Each line is valid JSON with resourceType == Patient
trace: R-HLT-066: large datasets paginated with NDJSON output (lower boundary = 1 record)
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests, time, json as _json

def test_bulk_export_single_record_produces_one_line_ndjson(
    fhir_base_url, authorised_headers, single_patient_tenant
):
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/$export",
        params={"_type": "Patient"},
        headers={**authorised_headers, "Accept": "application/fhir+json", "Prefer": "respond-async"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    poll_url = resp.headers["Content-Location"]
    manifest = <poll_until_complete>(poll_url, authorised_headers, timeout=30)
    patient_outputs = [o for o in manifest["output"] if o["type"] == "Patient"]
    assert len(patient_outputs) == 1, f"Expected 1 Patient output, got {len(patient_outputs)}"
    ndjson_resp = requests.get(patient_outputs[0]["url"], headers=authorised_headers)
    assert ndjson_resp.status_code == 200
    lines = [l for l in ndjson_resp.text.splitlines() if l.strip()]
    assert len(lines) == 1, f"Expected 1 NDJSON line, got {len(lines)}"
    record = _json.loads(lines[0])
    assert record["resourceType"] == "Patient"

tests.name: test_bulk_export_duplicate_kickoff_while_job_in_flight_rejected
preconditions: Client is provisioned with bulk-export scope
No existing in-flight export for this client
actions: Immediately GET /$export again (second kick-off) without cancelling the first
Capture HTTP status code of the second request
expected: Second request returns 429 or 409 indicating conflicting state transition
trace: R-HLT-066: conflicting state transitions must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests

def test_bulk_export_duplicate_kickoff_while_job_in_flight_rejected(
    fhir_base_url, authorised_headers
):
    export_headers = {
        **authorised_headers,
        "Accept": "application/fhir+json",
        "Prefer": "respond-async",
    }
    first = requests.get(f"{fhir_base_url}/$export", headers=export_headers)
    assert first.status_code == 202, f"First kick-off failed: {first.status_code}"
    second = requests.get(f"{fhir_base_url}/$export", headers=export_headers)
    assert second.status_code in (409, 429), (
        f"Expected 409/429 for duplicate kick-off, got {second.status_code}: {second.text}"
    )
    body = second.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    <cancel_export_job>(first.headers.get("Content-Location"), authorised_headers)

tests.name: test_bulk_export_missing_output_format_returns_400
preconditions: Client is provisioned with bulk-export scope
Request deliberately omits the _outputFormat parameter which the server mandates
actions: GET /$export with Accept header explicitly set to an unsupported media type and without _outputFormat
Capture HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body is an OperationOutcome with severity error
Diagnostics message references the missing or invalid element
trace: R-HLT-066: missing mandatory elements must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests

def test_bulk_export_missing_output_format_returns_400(
    fhir_base_url, authorised_headers
):
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/$export",
        params={"_outputFormat": ""},
        headers={
            **authorised_headers,
            "Accept": "application/fhir+json",
            "Prefer": "respond-async",
        },
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i.get("severity") == "error" for i in issues), (
        f"No error-severity issue found in: {issues}"
    )
    diagnostics = " ".join(i.get("diagnostics", "") for i in issues).lower()
    assert "_outputformat" in diagnostics or "output" in diagnostics, (
        f"Diagnostics do not mention missing element: {diagnostics}"
    )
